entries.message: The temples stand silent. Without faith, your subjects no longer believe you are chosen to rule.
The clergy proclaim you a prophet. Within a season, they govern the realm from the pulpit.
Starvation and rage empty the streets — then fill them with rebels. Your reign ends at the scaffold.
Loved to excess, the crowd elevates a champion of their own and casts you aside.
Your banners fall. With no army to defend the borders, foreign spears take the capital.
The army staged a military coup.
The vaults are bare. Debts come due, allies vanish, and the crown is sold for coin.
So much gold gathers that the merchant houses buy every loyalty — including yours.
